title: Eating Out
goal: Order food and drinks in a restaurant and ask for the bill.

completionCriteria.summary: Finish the quiz with at least 80%.

vocabulary.example: Can I see the menu, please?
Can I have the bill, please?
We have a table for two.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: Good evening. A table for two, please.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: Of course. Here is your table.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: Thank you. Can I see the menu, please?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: Here you are. Are you ready to order?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: Yes. I want the chicken and rice.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: Would you like something to drink?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: A glass of water, please. Is the soup spicy?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: Yes, it is a little spicy.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: The food is delicious! Can I have the bill?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: Sure, I will bring the bill now.

multipleChoice.prompt: What do you read to choose your food?
The person who serves your food is the...
What do you ask for when you want to pay?
multipleChoice.explanation: The bill shows how much to pay.

multipleChoice.prompt: The food tastes very good. It is...
multipleChoice.explanation: Delicious means it tastes very good.

multipleChoice.prompt: If food has a lot of chili, it is...
multipleChoice.explanation: Spicy means it has a lot of chili.

fillBlank.explanation: The menu lists the food you can order.
The bill shows how much you must pay.

matching.instruction: Match the word to its meaning.
Match the word to its meaning.
